name: Unified Genesys Dialer
description: A custom Lightning Web Component (LWC) built in Salesforce that integrates the Unified Genesys CTI dialer with Gong Engage. Reps access their Gong to-do call tasks and manage inbound queue routing directly from the Salesforce UI (Outbound & Inbound dialing with single/unified dialer).

updates.text: UAT kicked off. FC2 environment targeting ready Apr 23. All 3 dialer scenarios demoed in sandbox — disposition modal (60-sec auto-submit) approved by Chris and Alex Coventry.
Dialer scenarios finalized — 3 use cases confirmed (outbound 1-to-1, inbound queue, inbound 1-to-1). Disposition required, 60-sec auto-submit agreed. Role-specific options logged as fast follower.
Gong dispositions review completed with Austin Owens. Duplicate call logging blocker escalated to Gong with full context.
Engage dialer approach reviewed — three options evaluated (Single Genesys CTI, Gong native, hybrid). Single Genesys CTI approach selected as preferred path.
Discovery complete. Genesys CTI integration via custom Salesforce LWC approach confirmed with Gong team.

actions.title: Complete UAT — validate all dialer scenarios in FC2 environment
Decide on Gong dialing disable vs keep for pilot cohort
Gather and validate pilot user list (70-80 users) for Vishwas
Develop pilot evaluation criteria and rep satisfaction survey

past.title: Unified Genesys Dialer UAT
past.attendees: Alexander Sohn, Chris Hayden, Alex Coventry, Vishwas Dhudum
decisions: UAT environment (FC2) targeting ready Apr 23 2PM PST — official UAT begins Friday Apr 24
Build demonstrated in sandbox: LWC routes Gong to-do tasks and inbound queue through Genesys CTI within Salesforce
Disposition modal confirmed: 60-second auto-submit, close button disabled, reps cannot exit without selecting or waiting
To-do task list shows 3 at a time sorted by due date — task filtering/sorting logged as fast follower
Reps auto-authenticated into Genesys on Salesforce login (requires Genesys onboarding and call center assignment on user record)
Decision on disabling Gong dialing in extension tabled — separate meeting with Chris and Alex Coventry to decide
Pilot cohort: volunteer teams from GNR and/or acquisitions who have complained about current dialer — AB test against control group
Production deployment held until after Zero retreat (end of week of Apr 28)
Post-MVP: TCPA/DNC service call to filter only callable numbers before display in to-do list

actionItems.title: Vishwas to complete FC2 environment setup

Chris Hayden to compile list of rep feedback and enhancement requests from UAT
Alexander, Chris, Alex Coventry to meet to decide on Gong dialing disable vs keep
Vishwas to check recording button behavior for standard rep profiles

past.title: Vishwas & Alex Weekly Gong Sync
decisions: Reviewed UAT test scripts ahead of UAT session — expected outcomes confirmed
FC2 (Salesforce), Zillow Dev (Genesys), dedicated sandbox (Gong) are the UAT environments
Production deployment to be held until after Zero retreat (end of Apr 28 week)
Pilot users (70-80) will have two manual setup steps: permission set assignment + Gong user ID update in Salesforce — to be automated post-MVP (Ticket 11694)
Inbound UAT: users must be added to Genesys queues by Bree — ServiceNow ticket already created for 3 UAT users

actionItems.title: Vishwas to complete FC2 environment setup targeting Apr 23 2PM PST
Alexander to gather pilot user list and send to Vishwas (email or name format)
Alexander to create post-MVP ticket to automate permission set + Gong user ID assignment
Alexander to ask Bree about SalesLoft caller ID behavior for comparison
Alexander to start Slack thread with Vishwas + Bree to coordinate Genesys queue setup

past.title: Dialer Scenarios
past.attendees: Alexander Sohn, Chris Hayden, Vishwas Dhudum, Alex Coventry
decisions: Confirmed 3 dialer use cases: (1) outbound 1-to-1, (2) inbound queue calls, (3) inbound 1-to-1 calls. 4th noted: outbound calls to support/transfer hub
Disposition/wrap-up codes will be displayed consistently across ALL call types using the same custom model
Disposition will be REQUIRED — modal cannot be closed without selecting a value
60-second timeout after call completion before auto-submit with a default value
Genesys native wrap-up popup to be suppressed (1-second timeout) to avoid double-dispositioning
Disposition values will write back to corresponding Salesforce task
Options stored as custom metadata in Salesforce — configurable by admin, not hard-coded
Role/business-line-specific disposition options noted as future backlog item (not in current scope)

actionItems.title: Alex Coventry to provide core default disposition values (target: ~4 options, keep simple)
Alex Coventry to provide ideal disposition options by role (acquisitions, GNR, CSMs) for future enhancement
Vishwas to validate whether inbound call dispositions would impact outbound analytics in Gong
Vishwas to confirm disposition data writeback to Salesforce task in production (sandbox showed limited data)
Alexander to create story for Salesforce task disposition writeback
Configure 60-second auto-submit with non-selectable default value in Genesys
Suppress Genesys native wrap-up popup (1-second timeout)
Chris Hayden to ask reps about inbound call dispositioning preferences

name: Gong Engage Implementation
description: Phased rollout of Gong Engage across all ZG sales teams — replacing Salesloft. ASA goes live in two waves (Jun 8 & Jun 22) with New Construction, ZGMI/Mortech, and StreetEasy following in Phase 2 (July+). Full cutover complete by Salesloft offboarding on Aug 26, 2026.

updates.text: Salesforce CRM integration 70% complete — on track for May 1 target.
Rentals pilot sync completed with Michelle Maenpaa. Agreed to parallel path: SalesLoft extension exploration + Gong Engage pilot scoping. Follow-up scheduled week of May 4.
ASA pilot active. Office hours running to support reps on Engage & Forecast troubleshooting. Adoption tracking well.
Agent Sales rollout plan reviewed and aligned with Alex Coventry and Emily Bates. Business unit sequencing confirmed.
Go/No-Go meeting completed with full stakeholder group. Green light given for ASA rollout to proceed.

past.title: Rentals Gong Engage Pilot
past.attendees: Alexander Sohn, Michelle Maenpaa, Rentals team
decisions: Too early to commit to a Rentals pilot — Michelle just started and needs time to understand the team's current workflows and tool usage
Agreed to parallel path: (1) SalesLoft contract extension exploration and (2) Rentals pilot planning for future consideration
SalesLoft contract ends August 2026 — Rentals will be the last remaining group if all others migrate to Gong Engage by that date
Rentals would have its own separate Gong workspace with admin control if/when they migrate
Agreed on bi-weekly 1:1 cadence between Alexander and Michelle going forward
Alexander will audit Gong smart trackers and reallocate equitably across business lines (ASA currently consuming majority)
Gong moving to usage-based smart trackers — capacity concerns should resolve naturally
